title: Autonomous Mobile Robot with Semantic Mapping
highlights: Develop an autonomous indoor inspection robot using ROS2, SLAM Toolbox, and Nav2, with thermal and RGB-D cameras to detect and classify fire hazards in real time.
Engineer a multi-sensor fusion pipeline combining LiDAR SLAM, EKF odometry, and YOLOv8 object detection on a Raspberry Pi-powered mecanum drive platform.
Build a natural language AI interface running Qwen via Ollama on a laptop, injecting live robot context into the LLM to let operators query status and issue navigation commands in plain English.

title: Autonomous Shooting System
highlights: Developed autonomous target shooting system using Python and OpenCV on Raspberry Pi for real-time green LED detection and engagement with 90% accuracy under variable lighting.
Implemented PID control algorithm with position smoothing and dead zone compensation for 2-axis servo gimbal, achieving stable tracking with minimal oscillation.
Integrated trajectory compensation using fixed offset calibration and multi-method HSV colour detection (3 parallel algorithms) for robust target acquisition at 100cm distance.
Designed firing control system with GPIO motor control and automated target sequencing.

title: AI Multi-Model Object Detection System
highlights: Developed and trained a YOLOv11n object detection model to identify beverage bottles with 90-94% confidence using a custom dataset of 100+ labeled images.
Engineered a multi-model AI system integrating YOLO with an HSV-based color classification algorithm to detect and categorize bottles by cap color (yellow vs. white/clear) with 82-99% accuracy.
Implemented end-to-end machine learning pipeline including data collection, annotation using Roboflow, model training with PyTorch, and real-time inference deployment.
Optimized color detection algorithm to analyze specific object regions (cap area) for enhanced classification performance while maintaining minimal processing overhead.

title: Designed and Manufactured an Automated Trolley
highlights: Collaborated with a multidisciplinary team in the successful development of a practical, automated trolley solution for industrial and manufacturing applications.
Employed SOLIDWORKS for the initial design phase of the automated trolley, ensuring precision and efficiency in the development process.
Addressed challenges in loading/unloading mechanisms, straight-line movement, and 90° turns, showcasing a practical understanding of engineering applications.

title: Designed a Cleaning Device for the elderly people of Hong Kong
highlights: Conducted research with a team of seven to develop a user-friendly cleaning device for elderly residents in Hong Kong.
Utilized design tools including functional decomposition diagrams, mind maps, and concept sketches for ideation.
Developed a SOLIDWORKS prototype incorporating product architecture, component selection, and material optimization.
Presented the accessibility, ease of use, and effectiveness of the cleaning device to an audience.

title: Designed an Innovative Rehabilitation Glove for Tremor Patients
highlights: Led a team in designing a rehab glove with slider tendon actuators and a magnetic vibration absorber for physical therapy.
Incorporated haptic sensors for enhanced grip strength, allowing patients to lift heavy objects based on muscle movements.
Implemented a vibration absorber with magnetic field generation for stabilizing limbs affected by tremors.
Presented innovative rehab gloves, showcasing strong communication skills and effectively highlighting product features.

title: Designed 3D Vector Calculation Program in Python
highlights: Created a Python app for 3D vector calculations with object-oriented design.
Implemented secure login requiring username-password authentication, allowing three attempts before shutdown.
Designed a text menu interface for easy input of 3D vectors, enabling calculations like dot products and arctan, with a convenient application termination option.

title: Redesigned a Table Fan
highlights: Collaborated with a team of seven people to analyze and redesign table fan components for improved performance and manufacturability, based on analysis and market research.
Employed hand-drawn sketches and CAD models in SOLIDWORKS for detailed prototyping and stress simulation.
Assembled all components of the original fan in SOLIDWORKS, allowing for a comprehensive view of the product.

title: Instructed Children in Machine Learning and Programming with Emphasis on AI Recognition
highlights: Educated high school students in South Africa on Machine Learning and Computer Programming through Zoom.
Guided students in applying AI recognition to aid the visually impaired, utilizing Scratch and Google's Teachable Machine to build an Object Recognition System.

title: Designed a Gripper with a Water Bottle
highlights: Created a volume-defined bottle design using SOLIDWORKS.
Enhanced gripper for efficient bottle grasping, employing mechanical design principles.
Generated renderings with varied appearances and utilized image mapping and animation videos to showcase the bottle's gripping mechanism.
Produced precise 2D drawings of components with title blocks and annotations.

title: Assembled a Robot Arm
highlights: Designed and developed six degrees of Freedom Robot Arm using SOLIDWORKS.
Designed the Cover, Base, Shoulder Support, Upper Arm Model, Power Cable, and the Server Motor Subassembly.
Assembled all the created parts along with the predesigned parts to create the Robot Arm.
